goal: AGENTS.md + README: turns honest-signal section + log/show/trace trichotomy table

summary.title: AGENTS.md turns honest-signal section + README log/show/trace trichotomy
summary.what: AGENTS.md template: new "Turns and intent history" section explicitly states turns are a thinking scaffold for seal preparation, not a real-time activity log. "While working" section rewritten to encourage seal-preparation turns rather than per-change turns. README: new log/show/trace mini-table at top of Daily commands establishes the trichotomy (what exists / what decided / how unfolded). Full daily commands table updated to include trace, abandon, gaps, and the --allow-dirty seal flag that landed in recent PRs but were missing from README. Downstream IDE stubs and CLAUDE.md regenerated via init --rewire.
summary.why: Two problems addressed in one PR. (1) The "after each meaningful change record one turn" line in AGENTS.md was aspirational and contradicted by my own dogfood data — every intent has all turns at the same timestamp. The honest framing matches reality and matches the trace command output. (2) trace and abandon were not in README, so a new user reading the docs would not discover them. The trichotomy table is the user-suggested framing that makes trace feel natural rather than bolted-on.
summary.user_goal: New users (humans + agents) read coherent docs that match the implementation behaviour. Existing batch-write turn pattern is acknowledged rather than papered over.

decisions.point: Replace "After each meaningful change" vs add a caveat to it
decisions.rationale: The original text would always be a lie (no agent works that way); a caveat would just confuse. Direct rewrite to match how turns actually get used.

decisions.point: Mini trichotomy table at top of Daily commands vs replace full table
decisions.chose: mini at top + full table below
decisions.rationale: Per user spec: "small table to make trace feel natural, not bolted on". Keeping the full daily list below means readers still get a complete reference; the trichotomy is the headline.

decisions.point: Whether to bump AGENTS.md template version (7 → 8)
decisions.rationale: Existing AGENTS.md upsert tests assert version 7. Body changes within a major version are routine and the upsert logic preserves user content via section markers regardless. Version bump is reserved for breaking-shape changes that would orphan downstream caches.

decisions.point: Commit the regenerated IDE stubs (CLAUDE.md, .cursor/, etc.) or ignore them
decisions.rationale: They are committed in this repo (not gitignored) and reflect the canonical template state. Leaving them un-regenerated would diverge from what other contributors would see after rewire. The regeneration is the canonical mechanism so its output is the canonical state.

rejected.alternative: Keep "after each meaningful change" but soften to "after each meaningful change OR right before seal"
rejected.reason: Hedge language; readers cherry-pick the half that justifies their behaviour. The data shows nobody splits per-change in practice; commit to the honest framing.

rejected.alternative: Move all turn discipline into a separate doc and link from AGENTS.md
rejected.reason: AGENTS.md is the doc agents read; pushing it elsewhere reduces discoverability.

rejected.alternative: Add a doctor check that warns when same-second turns appear
rejected.reason: rc7 explicitly says: same-second turns are NORMAL, not a warning. Doctor surfacing it would re-introduce the judgment-not-information stance the spec rejected.

risks: Soft framing ("record turns at points that help YOU write a good seal") might be too loose — some agents could interpret it as license to record zero turns. Mitigated by the seal flow itself, which prompts for a turn list and would produce poorer summary if there are no turns to draw from.
README diff is large because the existing daily commands table grew by 4 rows. Reviewers should focus on the new trichotomy block and the trace/abandon/gaps additions; the rest is incidental.
followups: mainline status enhancement (separate PR per the user spec): show unsealed intents on other branches + recent sealed intents from team + actionable suggestions block. The user explicitly prioritized status over hooks for the next iteration.
When this PR lands, friend MVP rollout users will see the consistent trichotomy in their auto-installed AGENTS.md.

architectural_claims: Turns are documented as a seal-preparation scaffold; same-second batched turns are normal and surfaced honestly via the trace command.
log / show / trace form a documented trichotomy — list / conclusion / timeline — with one-line answers each.
behavioral_changes: New AGENTS.md regenerations (via init or init --rewire) write the new "Turns and intent history" section and the rewritten "While working" framing.
README Daily commands table includes trace, abandon, gaps, and the --allow-dirty seal flag.
